content_markdown:
The graph shows how the volume of a system varies with pressure during a cycle ABCA.

What is the work done in joules during the change AB?

diagram_metadata.description: A pressure-volume graph showing a cycle ABCA. The y-axis is pressure in units of $10^5$ Pa and the x-axis is volume in $m^3$. Point A is at $(1, 5)$, point B is at $(4, 5)$, and point C is at $(1, 2)$. Process AB is a horizontal line at constant pressure, BC is a curve, and CA is a vertical line at constant volume.

vibe_explanation: Work done in a thermodynamic process is given by the area under the P-V curve, or $W = P\Delta V$ for constant pressure processes. For the change AB, the pressure is constant at $5 \times 10^5$ Pa. The change in volume $\Delta V$ is $4\text{ m}^3 - 1\text{ m}^3 = 3\text{ m}^3$. Therefore, $W = (5 \times 10^5 \text{ Pa}) \times (3 \text{ m}^3) = 15 \times 10^5 \text{ J}$.
